examples.title: Process State Transition Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how process state changes based on events
examples.code:
// Simulating Process State Transitions
ENUM State { NEW, READY, RUNNING, BLOCKED, TERMINATED }

STRUCT Process {
 int pid
 State currentState
 PCB block
}

FUNCTION dispatch(processList):
 // Move process from READY to RUNNING
 FOR each process IN processList:
 IF process.currentState == READY:
 process.currentState = RUNNING
 RETURN process
 RETURN NULL

FUNCTION handleIORequest(runningProcess):
 // Running → Blocked
 runningProcess.currentState = BLOCKED
 ADD to IOWaitingQueue(runningProcess)
 RETURN NULL // CPU now idle

FUNCTION handleIOComplete(waitingProcess):
 // Blocked → Ready
 waitingProcess.currentState = READY
 REMOVE from IOWaitingQueue(waitingProcess)
 ADD to ReadyQueue(waitingProcess)

FUNCTION preempt(runningProcess):
 // Running → Ready (time slice expired)
 runningProcess.currentState = READY
 ADD to ReadyQueue(runningProcess)
 RETURN NULL
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the key state transitions: dispatch moves Ready→Running, I/O request causes Running→Blocked, I/O completion causes Blocked→Ready, and preemption causes Running→Ready. These are the fundamental events in process state management.

examples.title: PCB Context Save/Restore
examples.description: Shows how context switching saves and restores process state
examples.code:
// Context Switching in Process Management

FUNCTION contextSwitch(fromProcess, toProcess):
 // Save current process context to its PCB
 fromProcess.pcb.programCounter = CPU.programCounter
 fromProcess.pcb.accumulator = CPU.accumulator
 fromProcess.pcb.registers = CPU.allRegisters
 fromProcess.pcb.stackPointer = CPU.stackPointer
 fromProcess.pcb.state = READY
 
 // Restore new process context from its PCB
 CPU.programCounter = toProcess.pcb.programCounter
 CPU.accumulator = toProcess.pcb.accumulator
 CPU.allRegisters = toProcess.pcb.registers
 CPU.stackPointer = toProcess.pcb.stackPointer
 toProcess.pcb.state = RUNNING

// Example: Timer interrupt handling
FUNCTION timerInterrupt():
 // Save current running process
 currentProcess = getRunningProcess()
 contextSwitch(currentProcess, nextProcess)
 // Schedule next process from ready queue
 scheduleNext()
examples.explanation: Context switching involves two phases: saving the current process state to its PCB (so it can resume later) and loading the next process's state from its PCB. This is triggered by timer interrupts for preemption or when a running process blocks itself.

examples.title: Process Creation and Termination
examples.description: Demonstrates process lifecycle from fork to exit
examples.code:
// Process Creation and Termination Flow

FUNCTION createProcess(program):
 newPCB = allocatePCB()
 newPCB.pid = generateUniquePID()
 newPCB.parentPID = currentProcess.pid
 newPCB.state = NEW
 newPCB.programCounter = program.entryPoint
 
 // Load program into memory
 loadIntoMemory(newPCB, program)
 
 newPCB.state = READY
 ADD to ReadyQueue(newPCB)
 RETURN newPCB.pid

FUNCTION terminateProcess(process):
 process.state = TERMINATED
 closeAllFiles(process)
 releaseMemory(process)
 releaseResources(process)
 
 // Notify parent via wait() status
 SET exitStatus(process)
 
 IF parentWaiting:
 parent.process.state = READY
 ADD to ReadyQueue(parent)

// Parent process
FUNCTION forkAndExecute():
 childPid = createProcess(childProgram)
 // Parent continues execution
 
 // Wait for child to complete
 childStatus = wait(childPid) // Blocks parent
 // child becomes zombie until wait called
 cleanupChild(childPid)
examples.explanation: This demonstrates the complete process lifecycle: createProcess sets up PCB and moves to Ready state. terminateProcess moves to Terminated, releases resources but keeps exit status. The parent calling wait() reaps the zombie child, completing the lifecycle.
